claudeMd:
# Bug Fix: nginx MIME type override causes blank app

You are fixing a critical nginx configuration bug in a Vite + TypeScript canvas game ("Quantum Runner") served via Docker + nginx.

## Root Cause

In `nginx.conf`, the `types { application/javascript js mjs; }` block on lines 9–11 **completely replaces** all MIME types loaded by the preceding `include /etc/nginx/mime.types;`. Per nginx docs, `types {}` is a replacement, not a merge. This means CSS files are NOT served as `text/css`, causing browsers to refuse to apply the stylesheet. The app appears blank/broken.

## What to Fix

### `nginx.conf` — Remove the `types` override block

Remove lines 9–11 (the `types { ... }` block). Keep the `include /etc/nginx/mime.types;` line — it already maps `.js` and `.mjs` to `application/javascript` in the standard nginx distribution.

**Current (broken):**
```nginx
# Proper MIME types for JS modules
include /etc/nginx/mime.types;
types {
    application/javascript js mjs;
}
```

**Fixed:**
```nginx
# MIME types (includes JS, CSS, and all standard types)
include /etc/nginx/mime.types;
```

That is the ONLY change needed. Do not modify any other files.

## Verification

1. Run `npm run build` — must succeed
2. Verify nginx.conf no longer has a `types { }` block
3. Verify `include /etc/nginx/mime.types;` is still present

## Conventions

- Conventional Commits: `fix(deploy): remove nginx types override that broke CSS MIME type`
- Stage only `nginx.conf`
- Do NOT modify any other files
- Do NOT add new dependencies

checks: The page has a dark background (#0a0a0f), not white or default browser gray — this confirms CSS is loading correctly
A large canvas displays the title screen with 'QUANTUM RUNNER' text in cyan/teal on the dark background
Below the canvas, game instructions are visible in yellow text
A pulsing '[ Press ENTER or Click to Start ]' prompt is visible at the bottom of the canvas
Below the canvas, 'Accept [Enter]' and 'Pass [Space]' buttons are styled with colored borders (green and orange respectively)
Clicking the canvas or pressing Enter starts the game, showing a grid-based game board with a diamond-shaped player (cyan), triangular enemies (orange), and an EXIT cell (green)
